questions.q: Who is the central hero of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The Ramayana narrates the life of Rama, prince of Ayodhya, as a model of dharma.

questions.q: Who is the wife of Rama in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Sita, princess of Mithila, is the devoted wife of Rama in the epic.

questions.q: Which devoted monkey deity is a key helper of Rama?
questions.explanation: Hanuman, the vanara devotee of Rama, plays a central role in helping rescue Sita.

questions.q: Who is the demon king of Lanka who abducts Sita?
questions.explanation: Ravana, the ten-headed king of Lanka, abducts Sita and is later slain by Rama.

questions.q: Into how many kandas (books) is the Ramayana traditionally divided?
questions.explanation: The Valmiki Ramayana is traditionally told in seven kandas.

questions.q: Which sage is credited as the author of the original Sanskrit Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The sage Valmiki, revered as the Adi Kavi, composed the Ramayana in Sanskrit.

questions.q: What is the name of Rama's birthplace and kingdom?
questions.explanation: Rama is the prince of Ayodhya, the capital of the Kosala kingdom.

questions.q: Who is Rama's loyal younger brother who accompanies him into exile?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana faithfully accompanies Rama and Sita throughout their forest exile.

questions.q: For how many years was Rama exiled to the forest?
questions.explanation: Rama was exiled to the forest for fourteen years at Kaikeyi's behest.

questions.q: What core virtue does Rama exemplify throughout the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Rama is upheld as the ideal embodiment of dharma, righteous conduct and duty.

questions.q: Which queen demanded that Rama be exiled so her own son could become king?
questions.explanation: Queen Kaikeyi used her boons to demand Rama's exile and the crowning of Bharata.

questions.q: Which of these is NOT one of the seven kandas of the Valmiki Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Bhishma Parva belongs to the Mahabharata; the seven Ramayana kandas include Bala, Aranya, and Sundara.

questions.q: Who was the father of Rama and king of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: King Dasharatha of Ayodhya was the father of Rama and his three brothers.

questions.q: Who was the king of Mithila and Sita's father?
questions.explanation: King Janaka of Mithila raised Sita and set the bow-bending test for her marriage.

questions.q: What feat did Rama accomplish to win Sita's hand in marriage?
options: He stringing and breaking Shiva's great bow
He defeated a thousand kings in battle
questions.explanation: Rama lifted and broke the mighty bow of Shiva at Janaka's swayamvara to win Sita.

questions.q: Which brother of Ravana defected to Rama's side?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, Ravana's righteous brother, joined Rama after counseling Ravana in vain.

questions.q: What was the golden deer that lured Rama away before Sita's abduction?
questions.explanation: The demon Maricha took the form of a golden deer to draw Rama away so Ravana could seize Sita.

questions.q: Which vanara king allied with Rama after Rama helped him regain his throne?
questions.explanation: Rama helped Sugriva defeat his brother Vali, and Sugriva then aided the search for Sita.

questions.q: Which kanda chiefly recounts Hanuman's leap to Lanka and meeting with Sita?
questions.explanation: The Sundara Kanda centers on Hanuman's journey to Lanka and his meeting with Sita.

questions.q: What was the great bridge built by the vanaras to reach Lanka called?
questions.explanation: The vanara army built the Setubandha, also called Rama Setu, across the sea to Lanka.

questions.q: Who was the noble vulture that tried to stop Ravana from abducting Sita?
questions.explanation: Jatayu, the aged vulture, fought Ravana to save Sita and died of his wounds.

questions.q: Who were the twin sons of Rama and Sita?
questions.explanation: Luva and Kusha, the twin sons of Rama and Sita, were taught the Ramayana by Valmiki.

questions.q: What is the traditional number of verses (shlokas) said to comprise the Valmiki Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The Valmiki Ramayana is traditionally said to contain about 24,000 verses.

questions.q: According to tradition, what event inspired Valmiki to utter the first shloka of the epic?
options: A hunter killing one of a pair of mating krauncha birds
A vision of the god Vishnu
questions.explanation: Valmiki's grief at a hunter killing a krauncha bird poured out as the first shloka in the anushtubh meter.

questions.q: Which kanda opens the Ramayana and recounts Rama's childhood and marriage?
questions.explanation: The Bala Kanda is the first book, covering Rama's birth, youth, and marriage to Sita.

questions.q: Who was Ravana's powerful son, a master of illusion, who nearly defeated Rama's forces?
questions.explanation: Indrajit, also called Meghanada, used illusory warfare and was eventually slain by Lakshmana.

questions.q: Which kanda, regarded by many scholars as a later addition, narrates Sita's banishment and Rama's final days?
questions.explanation: The Uttara Kanda, often viewed as a later appendix, tells of Sita's banishment and Rama's departure.

questions.q: What was the name of Ravana's chief queen who urged him to return Sita?
questions.explanation: Mandodari, Ravana's principal queen, repeatedly counseled him to return Sita and avoid ruin.

questions.q: Who was the demoness whose insult and mutilation set off the chain leading to Sita's abduction?
questions.explanation: Surpanakha, Ravana's sister, was disfigured by Lakshmana, prompting Ravana's revenge by abducting Sita.

questions.q: In which poetic meter is the bulk of the Valmiki Ramayana composed?
questions.explanation: The Ramayana is composed chiefly in the anushtubh meter of thirty-two syllables per verse.
